column1: Capital and Financial Accounts: Flows and Policy Measures
Background. The capital and financial account weakened in 2023. The composition shifted notably. Net
portfolio outflows rose by nearly 10 percentage points of GDP to 15 percent of GDP, driven by portfolio
investments abroad (11 percent of GDP). In contrast, net other investment outflows declined by
16 percentage points to 1 percent of GDP. Overseas investment declined and net reduction in liabilities
mostly by commercial banks following the introduction of QCB measures, moderated. In 2024Q2, net
portfolio outflows abated while net incurrence of other investment liabilities resumed.
